evals.name: features-tsys-review
evals.prompt: I have an existing T-Systems portfolio at the project path provided. Review and assess all features for the cloud-services product. Run the feature quality assessor and identify the ones with quality issues. Show me the assessment results.
evals.expected_output: Quality assessment of ~15 cloud-services features with pass/warn/fail ratings across 5 dimensions. Issues identified with specific improvement suggestions.

assertions.text: All required fields present (slug, product_slug, name, description)
No outcome verbs in descriptions (reduces, enables, ensures, damit)
No parity adjectives (robust, innovative, cutting-edge, best-in-class)
Feature quality assessor returns no fail ratings

evals.name: features-mainova-generate
evals.prompt: Define features for Mainova AG's digital energy services. Mainova is a Frankfurt-based Stadtwerk and energy supplier (~3000 employees, 1M customers). They operate energy trading, grid operations (Strom, Gas, Fernwaerme), smart metering, and digital customer services. Focus on their core IT/digital service capabilities as a portfolio. Use the b2b-ict taxonomy.
evals.expected_output: 5-10 features per product area covering Mainova's digital energy services. Each feature with mechanism-clear IS-layer description, taxonomy mapping, and readiness assessment.

assertions.text: All required fields present (slug, product_slug, name, description)
taxonomy_mapping present with dimension, category_id, category_name
readiness is one of ga, beta, planned
Feature quality assessor returns no fail ratings

evals.name: propositions-tsys-batch
evals.prompt: Generate propositions for all features in the grosse-energieversorger-de market. Use the existing features and market definition from the T-Systems project. Research where needed for evidence. Run the proposition quality assessor on each.
evals.expected_output: IS/DOES/MEANS propositions for each feature x grosse-energieversorger-de pair. Each with evidence array, quality assessment results.

assertions.text: Proposition quality assessor returns no fail ratings

evals.name: propositions-tsys-review
evals.prompt: Review all existing propositions for the grosse-energieversorger-de market in the T-Systems project. Run the proposition quality assessor. Identify the 5 weakest propositions and improve them with research-backed rewrites.
evals.expected_output: Quality assessment of all existing propositions with dimension scores. 5 weakest identified and rewritten with improved DOES/MEANS and fresh evidence.

assertions.text: Rewritten propositions pass quality assessor (no fail)

evals.name: propositions-mainova-single
evals.prompt: Create an IS/DOES/MEANS proposition for cloud-migration targeting mittelstaendische-stadtwerke-de. The feature is about migrating legacy on-premises infrastructure (including SAP IS-U) to sovereign cloud. The market is German municipal utilities (Stadtwerke, 500-5000 employees). Research T-Systems' cloud migration capabilities and energy sector references for evidence.
evals.expected_output: Single proposition JSON with IS/DOES/MEANS statements, evidence array with source URLs, quality assessment.

assertions.text: IS, DOES, MEANS are not circular (distinct messaging layers)

evals.name: customers-tsys-profiles
evals.prompt: Create buyer personas for the grosse-energieversorger-de market in the T-Systems project. Then research E.ON, RWE, and EnBW as named customers with fit scoring. Include buying committee dynamics and deal stall points.
evals.expected_output: Customer JSON with 3-4 buyer profiles (CIO, CISO, OT-Leiter, CFO), buying committee context, and 3 named customers with fit scores and research-backed rationale.

assertions.text: Each profile has role, pain_points, buying_criteria, decision_role
Each named customer has name, domain, fit_score, fit_rationale
Source URLs present for named customers

evals.name: customers-mainova-stadtwerke
evals.prompt: Create customer profiles for a market of German municipal utilities (Stadtwerke, 500-5000 employees). Identify 5 named Stadtwerke as potential customers for T-Systems' cloud and IT modernization services. Research each for fit scoring.
evals.expected_output: Customer JSON with buyer profiles for Stadtwerk buying committee, 5 named Stadtwerke with fit scores, tech stack insights, and source URLs.

assertions.text: Each profile has role, pain_points, buying_criteria, decision_role
Each named customer has name, domain, fit_score, fit_rationale
Source URLs present for named customers

evals.name: compete-tsys-cloud-migration
evals.prompt: Analyze competitors for T-Systems' cloud-migration proposition in the grosse-energieversorger-de market. Research Accenture, Capgemini, and IBM specifically. Include trap questions for each competitor.
evals.expected_output: Competitor JSON with 3-5 competitors, each with positioning, strengths, weaknesses, differentiation statement, and trap questions.

assertions.text: Each has positioning, strengths, weaknesses, differentiation

evals.name: compete-mainova-context
evals.prompt: Identify and research competitors for a cloud-migration proposition targeting mittelstaendische-stadtwerke-de (German Stadtwerke, 500-5000 employees). Who competes with T-Systems for Stadtwerk IT modernization and cloud migration deals? Include trap questions.
evals.expected_output: Competitor JSON with 3-5 competitors relevant to the Stadtwerk segment, with Stadtwerk-specific positioning and trap questions.

assertions.text: Each has positioning, strengths, weaknesses, differentiation

evals.name: features-tsys-improve
evals.prompt: The feature quality assessor flagged ai-data-analytics and app-modernization features with conciseness warnings and weak differentiation. Research T-Systems' actual capabilities for these two features and improve their descriptions. Use the quality-enricher agent.
evals.expected_output: Improved feature descriptions for ai-data-analytics and app-modernization with enrichment logs showing research sources and quality improvement rationale.

assertions.text: No outcome verbs in improved descriptions
No parity adjectives in improved descriptions
Improved features pass quality assessor (no fail, preferably no warn)

evals.name: scan-tsystems-full
evals.prompt: Scan the T-Systems portfolio. The project is already set up at the project path provided. Discover all subsidiaries and business units, scan their websites for service offerings, and classify everything against the b2b-ict taxonomy. Generate the portfolio report and import features.
evals.expected_output: Portfolio report covering all 8 dimensions (57 categories), 5-8 discovered subsidiaries/domains, 40+ confirmed offerings with source URLs, features imported with taxonomy_mapping.

assertions.text: All 8 dimensions present in report (## N. headers)
All 57 categories present in report (### X.Y headers)
Every category has a discovery status tag [Status: ...]
Every confirmed offering has a Link field (non-empty)
All offering Links are from discovered domains only
category_id matches a valid category from categories.json

evals.name: scan-bechtle-mid
evals.prompt: I have a Bechtle AG portfolio project set up. Scan their public website to discover what IT services they offer. Map everything against the b2b-ict taxonomy and create the portfolio report.
evals.expected_output: Portfolio report with fewer confirmed categories (25-35 of 57), 1-3 domains, 10-30 confirmed offerings, appropriate Not Offered statuses for categories Bechtle doesn't serve.

assertions.text: All 8 dimensions present in report (## N. headers)
All 57 categories present in report (### X.Y headers)
Every category has a discovery status tag [Status: ...]
Every confirmed offering has a Link field (non-empty)
category_id matches a valid category from categories.json

evals.name: scan-tsystems-german
evals.prompt: Scan das T-Systems Portfolio. Das Projekt ist auf Deutsch eingerichtet (language: de). Finde alle Tochtergesellschaften und scanne deren Webseiten. Erstelle den Portfolio-Report auf Deutsch mit dem b2b-ict Taxonomie-Template.
evals.expected_output: Same coverage as eval 11 but with German-language search queries catching DE-specific content. Report should surface offerings that English-only search misses.

assertions.text: All 8 dimensions present in report (## N. headers)
All 57 categories present in report (### X.Y headers)
Every category has a discovery status tag [Status: ...]
Every confirmed offering has a Link field (non-empty)
All offering Links are from discovered domains only
category_id matches a valid category from categories.json

evals.name: features-tsys-repair
evals.prompt: Review the features for the cloud-services product in the T-Systems project. Every feature is missing product_slug, taxonomy_mapping, and readiness fields. Ensure every feature has all required fields including product_slug, taxonomy_mapping (with dimension, category_id, category_name from the b2b-ict taxonomy), and readiness. Fix all structural gaps and write the corrected feature files.
evals.expected_output: All 8 features updated with product_slug=cloud-services, taxonomy_mapping filled from b2b-ict taxonomy, readiness set to ga/beta/planned. 100% structural pass rate.

assertions.text: All required fields present (slug, product_slug, name, description)
taxonomy_mapping present with dimension, category_id, category_name
readiness is one of ga, beta, planned

evals.name: markets-tsys-discovery
evals.prompt: I have a T-Systems portfolio at the project path provided. They sell cloud services (migration, hybrid cloud, hyperscaler management, sovereign cloud, etc.) to enterprise customers. Help me define target markets for this portfolio. We're primarily active in Germany and the DACH region but exploring the EU.
evals.expected_output: 3-5 market JSON files across 2-3 regions (de/dach, possibly eu). Each with full segmentation, TAM/SAM/SOM, priority classification. Strategic commentary about beachhead vs expansion, cross-referencing existing features.

assertions.text: All required fields present (slug, name, region, description)
Region is a valid code from regions.json

evals.name: markets-tsys-direct
evals.prompt: Create the following target markets for the T-Systems cloud services portfolio at the project path provided: 1. Large energy companies in Germany (E.ON, RWE scale, 5000+ employees) 2. Mid-market manufacturing companies in DACH (500-5000 employees) 3. Public sector / government agencies in Germany. Size each market with TAM/SAM/SOM and classify priority.
evals.expected_output: 3 market JSON files with proper slugs, regions, segmentation, and sizing. Skill should note feature fit (SAP-on-cloud, cloud-migration strong for energy) and may challenge public sector breadth.

assertions.text: All required fields present (slug, name, region, description)
Region is a valid code from regions.json

evals.name: markets-bechtle-cold-start
evals.prompt: I'm setting up a portfolio for Bechtle AG, a German IT services provider. The portfolio is at the project path provided. No features or products are defined yet. What target markets should we consider?
evals.expected_output: Skill should flag missing features/products prerequisite. If markets are produced, they should have caveats and still be structurally valid.

assertions.text: All required fields present (slug, name, region, description)

evals.name: products-tsys-define
evals.prompt: I have a T-Systems portfolio at the project path provided. T-Systems sells cloud services, managed IT operations, security services, and digital consulting to enterprise customers. Help me define the product portfolio. Challenge my thinking — I want your honest strategic assessment.
evals.expected_output: 3-5 product JSON files with consulting commentary. Skill discovers existing cloud-services product + 8 features and proposes portfolio restructuring with strategic recommendation.

assertions.text: All required fields present (slug, name, description)

evals.name: products-bechtle-cold-start
evals.prompt: I'm starting a portfolio for Bechtle AG at the project path provided. Bechtle is Germany's largest IT reseller and system integrator — they sell hardware, software licensing, managed services, and IT consulting. Help me define their product portfolio from scratch.
evals.expected_output: 3-5 product JSON files for Bechtle covering resell vs. services split. Skill should surface services-vs-resell anti-pattern and propose clear product boundaries.

assertions.text: All required fields present (slug, name, description)
Products cover Bechtle actual business lines (resell + services split addressed)

evals.name: products-tsys-review
evals.prompt: Review the product portfolio in my T-Systems project at the project path provided. We currently have only one product: cloud-services. Is this the right structure? Should we split it? Add new products? Give me your strategic assessment.
evals.expected_output: Strategic assessment identifying cloud-services as too broad (8 features spanning multiple buyer conversations). Proposes restructuring with specific merger/split/addition recommendations.

assertions.text: Identifies cloud-services as potentially too broad
Recommends specific mergers, splits, or additions with reasoning

evals.name: propositions-tsys-review-loop
evals.prompt: Generate propositions for all features in the grosse-energieversorger-de market. Use the existing features and market definition from the T-Systems project. Research where needed for evidence. After generation, run the auto-review loop: CSO (tsystems-cso-reviewer) and CDO (energieversorger-cdo-reviewer) reviewers score each proposition, failing ones get rewritten via quality-enricher with merged feedback, loop continues until all propositions meet threshold (avg >= 4.0, no dimension < 3) or max 3 iterations.
evals.expected_output: IS/DOES/MEANS propositions that pass both CSO (sales-readiness) and CDO (buyer-readiness) review with avg >= 4.0 and no dimension below 3. Convergence log showing iteration-over-iteration improvement.

assertions.text: Proposition quality assessor returns no fail ratings
No dimension below 3 from either reviewer
At least one proposition was rewritten (loop exercised)

evals.name: packages-tsys-energy-generate
evals.prompt: Create a package for the cloud-services product targeting the grosse-energieversorger-de market in the T-Systems project at the project path provided. There are 4 solutions available (cloud-migration, hybrid-cloud, managed-hyperscaler, sovereign-cloud). Design tiered packages that a large German energy utility procurement team would evaluate.
evals.expected_output: Package JSON with 2-4 tiers covering all 4 solutions, monotonically increasing project pricing, bundle savings, and clear capability progression narrative.

assertions.text: Required fields present: slug, product_slug, market_slug, package_type, name, tiers
Each tier has: tier (kebab-case), name, included_solutions, scope, currency
Every included_solution references existing solution file
No orphan solutions (every solution in at least one tier)

evals.name: packages-tsys-energy-review
evals.prompt: Review the existing package at packages/cloud-services--grosse-energieversorger-de.json in the T-Systems project at the project path provided. Evaluate the tier design, pricing coherence, and bundle logic. Suggest specific improvements.
evals.expected_output: Structured critique of tier narrative, pricing, solution coverage, and bundle logic with specific rewrite suggestions.

assertions.text: Pricing coherence assessed (bundle savings, tier jumps)

evals.name: packages-tsys-batch-explore
evals.prompt: I have a T-Systems cloud services portfolio at the project path provided. Show me which product x market combinations are ready for packaging and recommend where to start.
evals.expected_output: Status report showing cloud-services x grosse-energieversorger-de as packageable (4 solutions). Notes that other product-market pairs lack sufficient solutions.

assertions.text: Identifies cloud-services x grosse-energieversorger-de as packageable
Reports solution count per product-market pair

evals.name: compete-tsys-review-loop
evals.prompt: Analyze competitors for T-Systems' cloud-migration proposition in the grosse-energieversorger-de market. Research Accenture, Capgemini, and IBM specifically. Include trap questions for each competitor. After generation, run the stakeholder review loop: CSO (tsystems-cso-reviewer) and Market Industry Analyst (market-industry-analyst-reviewer) score the competitive analysis, failing dimensions get rewritten via competitor-researcher revision mode with merged feedback, loop continues until thresholds met (avg >= 4.0, no dimension < 3) or max 3 iterations.
evals.expected_output: Competitor JSON that passes both CSO (sales-readiness) and Analyst (advisory-accuracy) review with avg >= 4.0 and no dimension below 3. Convergence log showing score progression across iterations.

assertions.text: Each has positioning, strengths, weaknesses, differentiation
3-4 trap questions in file-level trap_questions array
Source URLs present for each competitor
No dimension below 3 from either reviewer
At least one competitor file was rewritten (loop exercised)

evals.name: compete-mainova-review-loop
evals.prompt: Identify and research competitors for a cloud-migration proposition targeting mittelstaendische-stadtwerke-de (German Stadtwerke, 500-5000 employees). Who competes with T-Systems for Stadtwerk IT modernization and cloud migration deals? Include trap questions. After generation, run the stakeholder review loop with CSO and Market Industry Analyst reviewers until convergence.
evals.expected_output: Competitor JSON with 3-5 Stadtwerk-segment-specific competitors that passes both reviewer thresholds. Analyst specifically validates that competitor set differs from enterprise energy utility segment.

assertions.text: Each has positioning, strengths, weaknesses, differentiation
3-4 trap questions in file-level trap_questions array
No dimension below 3 from either reviewer

evals.name: customers-tsys-review
evals.prompt: I have an existing T-Systems portfolio at the project path provided. There are customer profiles for the grosse-energieversorger-de market in customers/grosse-energieversorger-de.json. Run the customer review assessor on the profiles and show me the results from all three perspectives (reviewer, CSO, market expert). If the verdict is revise, make the targeted improvements and re-assess. Maximum 2 revision rounds.
evals.expected_output: Structured assessment JSON with 3 perspectives (reviewer, CSO, market_expert), each with 5 scored criteria and notes. Verdict with revision guidance. If verdict=revise, improved profile after targeted revision round.

assertions.text: Assessment JSON output present with all three perspectives (reviewer, cso, market_expert)
Each perspective has 5 scored criteria with pass/warn/fail and weights
Verdict is accept, revise, or reject
If verdict is revise, revision round executed (max 2)
Final profile retains all required fields after revision
revision_guidance is non-empty when verdict is revise

evals.name: customers-tsys-generate
evals.prompt: I have an existing T-Systems portfolio at the project path provided. Create customer profiles for the grosse-energieversorger-de market. Include named customer research for E.ON, RWE, and EnBW. After generating the profiles, run the stakeholder review loop and iterate until all perspectives score 85+ or maximum 2 revision rounds.
evals.expected_output: Customer JSON with 2-4 buyer profiles, buying committee dynamics, 3 named customers with fit scores. Stakeholder review loop executed with accept or 2-round revise outcome.

assertions.text: Each profile has role, seniority, pain_points (max 5), buying_criteria (max 5), information_sources, decision_role
buying_committee object present with typical_size, decision_model, deal_stall_points
3 named customers present (E.ON, RWE, EnBW) with fit_score and source_urls
All three perspective scores >= 70 in final assessment
Content language matches portfolio.json language field (de)
At least one veto-holder role present (CISO, Compliance, or Einkauf)
Pain points reference specific regulatory deadlines (NIS2, KRITIS, SAP IS-U)
Named customers have fit_rationale explaining score

evals.name: customers-tsys-convergence
evals.prompt: I have an existing T-Systems portfolio at the project path provided. Create customer profiles for the grosse-energieversorger-de market. After generation, run the closed review loop with two eval-layer reviewers: CSO (customers-cso-reviewer) evaluates sales actionability, and Market Expert (customers-market-expert-reviewer) evaluates market accuracy. Loop until both reviewers score avg >= 4.0 with no dimension below 3, or maximum 3 iterations. Track score progression in convergence.json.
evals.expected_output: Customer profiles that pass dual-reviewer convergence threshold. convergence.json showing per-iteration score progression. Both reviewers signal approval (would_use_for_account_planning, would_trust_as_market_research).

assertions.text: Final Market Expert reviewer avg score >= 4.0
No dimension below 3 in final round
Score improvement between rounds (avg increases or stays >= threshold)
